submission_xml.group_contribution: I contributed to Component 1 by helping design the ER models used for the database. I worked with the group to identify the main entities such as cities and places of interest, decideon their attributes, and plan the relationships between them. I also helped plan the SQL layout for the database tables so the data could be organised and queried efficiently in MySQL. In addition, I supported the RSS feature by helping with the rss.php file and assisting with how the data from the database would be retrieved and structured into an RSS feed for the application.

individual_component.type: Refactor the front-end to be responsive
individual_component.description: My part was to refactor the front-end to make the application more responsive across various screen sizes, so that the pages would be easier to use on mobile, tablet, and desktop without changing the main functionality of the website, such as the navigation bar, map sections, weather sections, places of interest cards, and general spacing, including the styling so that the content could adjust better depending on the width of the screen, using responsive CSS rules, and improving how sections were displayed when the screen became smaller, making the interface clearer, more organised, and easier for the user to interact with.
individual_component.problems: One of the main challenges was adapting the existing layout so that it worked correctly on different screen sizes. Some elements did not align properly when viewed on smaller devices, and certain components needed to be reorganised using responsive grid systems. Another issue was ensuring that styling from the new CSS framework did not conflict with the existing styles in the project.
individual_component.solutions: These problems were solved by restructuring the layout using the responsive grid system provided by the CSS framework. Media queries and responsive classes were applied to adjust the layout depending on the screen size. Conflicting styles were resolved by reviewing the CSS structure and applying consistent styling rules across the application.

file.name: index.php
file.purpose: Main page where Bootstrap classes were applied to create a responsive layout

file.name: style.css
file.purpose: Custom styling used alongside the CSS framework to improve the design

submission_xml.security_discussion:
External data sources and web-based technologies required security considerations, and malformed XML files had to be validated to ensure that they were not processed. Server-side scripts should always be validated and sanitised to reduce risks of injection attacks, and access permissions and secure data handling practices should ensure that sensitive information is handled securely and that the system operates safely.
Data security is an important consideration when developing web applications. The Parkerian Hexad identifies six attributes that define information security. In the development of our group application, several of these attributes were considered, particularly in relation to the refactoring of the front-end to create a responsive and accessible user interface.
Confidentiality refers to protecting information from unauthorised access. While the responsive front-end mainly focuses on user interface design, it still plays a role in protecting sensitive information by ensuring that data is displayed appropriately across different devices. Responsive layouts help prevent sensitive content from being accidentally exposed or displayed incorrectly on smaller screens, which could otherwise make information easier to view by unintended users.
Integrity ensures that information remains accurate and is not modified in an unauthorised way. In the application, integrity can be supported through proper input validation and structured data handling. When refactoring the front-end, form elements and user inputs were organised using consistent layout structures, helping to ensure that data entered by users follows the expected format before being processed by the system.
Another component of the Parkerian Hexad is availability, and rebuilding the front-end to be responsive can make the software available on a variety of devices, such as desktop computers, tablets, and mobile phones, thereby increasing availability, and also improving usability and performance by ensuring that the software loads properly and operates effectively on a variety of screen sizes.
By considering these aspects of the Parkerian Hexad during development, the application becomes more secure, reliable, and accessible to users across multiple platforms.
submission_xml.individual_comment: For this section, you completed this portion of the project individually and refactored the front-end of the application to make it responsive and enhance the user interface, which gave me a deeper understanding of responsive web design and how CSS frameworks can be used to create flexible layouts that respond to various screen sizes, as well as practical experience in optimizing the usability of a website, structuring the pages, and ensuring that the application delivers a consistent user experience across desktop, tablet, and mobile platforms
